original_question:
Which statement is sarcastic?
Options:
(A) His reaction is understandable—he didn't get a reply in 2 years
(B) His reaction is understandable—he didn't get a reply in 2 minutes
subquestion: Generate an example of a sarcastic comment that a coworker might make when they are asked to redo a task that they already completed correctly the first time. The comment should include the phrase "no problem."
answer: "No problem, I'd love to redo the entire project from scratch just to prove to you that I did it right the first time. I mean, who needs efficiency or time management when I can just redo the same work twice?"
judge_answer:
The given answer is trying to convey sarcasm, but it doesn't quite capture the tone and style of a coworker's comment in a professional setting. The comment seems more confrontational and argumentative rather than sarcastic and passive-aggressive.

A more fitting example of a sarcastic comment might be: "No problem, I was just twiddling my thumbs, waiting for the opportunity to redo the same task I already completed yesterday. Thanks for the chance to waste some more time."

This revised comment still conveys the sarcasm, but in a more subtle and less confrontational manner.

FINAL ANSWER: incorrect
